title: Does K_50 decompose into 7 copies of the Hoffman-Singleton graph?
micro_paper_assessment: A real theorem-facing slice is now visible, but the solve did not settle the exact decomposition or obstruction and remains too thin for the micro-paper lane on its own.
family_affinity: Exceptional Moore-graph decomposition / strongly regular color-class decomposition of a complete graph.
generalization_signal: The local block-matching argument scales to hypothetical decompositions of K_{d^2+1} into d triangle-free Moore graphs, but global closure here still depends on the concrete 14-regular residue.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any seven-copy Hoffman-Singleton decomposition of K_50 forces a rigid vertex-centered 7x7 block system with pairwise perfect matchings between corresponding color blocks, and every edge lies in exactly 30 rainbow triangles.
next_action: Obtain the explicit five-copy packing or its 14-regular residual graph and test completion/obstruction against the forced block-matching and rainbow-triangle constraints.
